stem: In the xy-plane, the graph of the linear function f contains the points (0, 2) and (6, 26). Which equation defines f, where y = f(x)?
rationale: Choice D is correct. Slope = (26 - 2)/(6 - 0) = 24/6 = 4. The line passes through (0, 2), so the y-intercept is 2. Therefore f(x) = 4x + 2. Choice A uses (26 - 2) as the slope directly. Choice B uses the x-coordinate as slope. Choice C uses the x-coordinate of (6, 26) as the slope.
